ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Rempah Udang; mapped to closest glutinous rice cake class. Rempah Udang is a Malay-style grilled cylindrical glutinous rice cake filled with dried shrimp and dried chilli.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (rice-based snack) used as closest defensible anchor; no specific Rempah Udang entry exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Rempah Udang or equivalent glutinous rice snack category.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of Rempah Udang is approximately one piece (about 50 g), which is a single cylindrical glutinous rice roll grilled in banana leaf.

nutrient_source.title: Rempah Udang — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Rempah Udang (CR ID F11090052). Iron value reported as -1 in source, indicating data not available.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of glutinous rice — analogous estimate based on Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Rempah Udang exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 98 is estimated from glutinous rice (sticky rice), which has a published GI of approximately 98 (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI × carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 98 × 16.1 / 100 ≈ 15.8 (based on 50 g serving providing approximately 16.1 g carbohydrate).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from belacan (shrimp paste) and dried shrimp; 363 mg sodium per 100 g with low potassium offset
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Rempah Udang to one small piece (≈50 g) per occasion, as the combined sodium from belacan and dried shrimp contributes approximately 182 mg sodium per serving, and the low potassium content provides minimal counterbalance to sodium-driven blood pressure elevation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Glutinous rice base with very high glycemic index (~98) and 32.2 g carbohydrate per 100 g with only 1.9 g dietary fibre
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat Rempah Udang as an occasional food only; the glutinous rice base has a GI of approximately 98 and delivers approximately 16 g rapidly absorbed carbohydrate per 50 g piece, causing sharp postprandial glucose spikes that are poorly buffered by the low fibre content of 1.9 g per 100 g.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from coconut (~3.8 g per 100 g) combined with high sodium (363 mg per 100 g) and refined carbohydrate load
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, Rempah Udang should be consumed sparingly due to its saturated fat content (~3.8 g per 100 g from coconut), elevated sodium (363 mg per 100 g), and high glycaemic load, all of which can adversely affect lipid profiles, blood pressure, and endothelial function when consumed regularly.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat from coconut and belacan, high sodium, refined glutinous rice carbohydrate, and nightshade ingredient (dried chilli)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit Rempah Udang as the combination of saturated fat (~3.8 g per 100 g), high sodium (363 mg per 100 g), and refined glutinous rice carbohydrate may promote inflammatory mediators, and the dried chilli (a nightshade) may aggravate symptoms in solanine-sensitive individuals.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Rempah Udang is a traditional Malay-Singaporean snack consisting of a cylindrical glutinous rice cake filled with a spiced dried shrimp (udang) mixture and grilled in banana leaf. The name literally means 'shrimp spice' or 'spiced shrimp' in Malay. The outer layer is made from glutinous (sticky) rice, which is soaked, seasoned, and wrapped tightly around a filling of dried shrimp, dried chilli, belacan (fermented shrimp paste), grated coconut, and aromatic spices including turmeric and lemongrass. The roll is then wrapped in banana leaf and grilled over charcoal or an open flame, imparting a subtle smoky, herbal aroma. The texture is characteristically chewy and dense from the glutinous rice, with a savoury, mildly spicy, and umami-rich filling from the dried seafood and belacan. Rempah Udang is commonly found at Malay kuih stalls, pasar malam (night markets), and festive occasions in Singapore and Malaysia. Nutritionally, each 100 g provides 202 kcal, 5.5 g protein, 5.7 g fat (approximately 3.8 g saturated from coconut), and 32.2 g carbohydrate with only 1.9 g dietary fibre. Sodium is notably high at 363 mg per 100 g due to belacan and dried shrimp. The glutinous rice base has a very high glycaemic index (~98), making this snack a high-glycaemic-load food unsuitable for frequent consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension. It is halal-certified and dairy-free but contains crustacean allergens from dried shrimp and belacan.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID F11090052), which is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. GI is estimated from the closest approved analogous food (glutinous rice, GI ~98) from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed publication. Saturated fat estimated from known coconut content of the recipe; detailed fatty acid breakdown not available in HPB source.
notes: iron_mg is set to -1: the HPB Singapore Foodo source (CR ID F11090052) reports iron as -1, indicating data not available for this traditional preparation. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid fractionation data for Rempah Udang are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. sugar_g is reported as 0.0 by HPB source (confirmed zero per HPB Foodo F11090052); this is plausible given the savoury filling and unsweetened glutinous rice base. saturated_fat_g of 3.8 g estimated from known coconut and belacan content of the recipe; HPB source does not separately report saturated fat for this item — this is an approximation and should be verified against a detailed recipe analysis. calcium_mg of 33.0 mg sourced directly from HPB Foodo F11090052.
